summary: Iteration 9 (Mock Interview gmail-verified gate, Skill Set required on slot, 'Your Total Experience' rename, Job-Post mandatory-field validations, open_positions Picker, mobile masking, missing_fields[10], alternate-gmail label, Job Posted Successfully card). Added /app/backend/tests/test_iteration9.py with 17 NEW iter-9 tests. Result: 13/17 GREEN, 4/17 FAILED. Full backend baseline (pre-iter9) re-verified: 136/136 GREEN. Combined run including iter9: 149/153 (136+13 / 136+17). The 4 failures are a SINGLE root cause BACKEND BUG: POST /api/jobs returns HTTP 422 (Pydantic validation envelope) for empty title / empty description / empty location / empty skills_required — instead of the iter-9-spec'd 400 with detail messages 'Job Title is required.' / 'Job Description is required.' / 'Location is required.' / 'Skill Set is required.'. RCA: JobPostBody (server.py:327-338) declares title/description/location with Field(min_length=2) and skills_required with Field(min_length=1) — FastAPI/Pydantic intercepts the request body BEFORE the handler runs and short-circuits with 422 and the Pydantic 'string_too_short'/'too_short' envelope. The handler's human-readable 400 branches at server.py:1368-1375 are dead code for empty values; they only fire for whitespace-padded inputs that happen to be >= 2 chars (test_skills_required_only_whitespace_400 confirms this — '   ' passes Pydantic and triggers the 400 path). Note the spec also requires a 'Company Name is required.' message but the handler currently auto-falls-back to poster's name/email when company is empty (server.py:1377-1380) so it never raises 400 for missing company; the iteration-9 spec calls out title/description/location/skill_set/open_positions as mandatory and is silent on company-required behavior, so we did not assert that case. Frontend smoke (mobile 390x844, signup → login as @acmecorp.io pro): /professional/post-job → tap 'Post job' with all fields empty → 4 inline red errors appear ('Job Title is required.', 'Job Description is required.', 'Location is required.', 'Skill Set is required.') — VERIFIED via document.innerText scrape AND screenshot. 'Number of Open Positions *' Picker renders with default '1 to 5' selected — VERIFIED (data-testid=pj-openings present, picker visible in screenshot). /professional/profile → Profile Incomplete (orange) banner renders with 7 missing items for a fresh pro (Mobile Number, Alternate Gmail Address, Company Name, Designation, Total Experience, Current Location, Skill Set, Profile Photo) — VERIFIED. Alternate Gmail label reads 'Alternate Gmail (Used for Communication)' (rendered uppercase via Input CSS as 'ALTERNATE GMAIL (USED FOR COMMUNICATION)') — VERIFIED in code at profile.tsx:271. Mobile-number masking helper maskPhone() implemented at profile.tsx:15-28 + Input value={editingPhone?phone:maskPhone(phone)} at profile.tsx:258 — code-VERIFIED. /professional/slots → 'YOUR TOTAL EXPERIENCE (YEARS)' label renders (NOT 'Candidate experience') — VERIFIED in screenshot. Gmail-verified gate: as a fresh non-gmail-verified pro, filling skills + tapping 'Create slot' opens the ConfirmDialog with title 'Gmail verification is required before creating a Mock Interview slot.' + 'Verify Gmail' / 'Cancel' buttons — VERIFIED via screenshot AND document.innerText. Tapping 'Verify Gmail' routes to /professional/profile — VERIFIED (page.url == /professional/profile). Backend 'success' branch: /api/interviews/slots with gmail_verified=true + skill_set=['React'] returns 200 with status:'available' — VERIFIED. /api/auth/me missing_fields[] returns canonical 10-factor set; full profile + gmail-verified clears the list to [] — VERIFIED. /pro/gmail/verify-otp drops 'Alternate Gmail Address' from missing_fields — VERIFIED. open_positions_label round-trip ('1 to 5','1 to 10','1 to 50','1 to 100','100+') persists to DB and is returned on GET /api/jobs — VERIFIED. Legacy numeric open_positions (without label) accepted, label defaults to '1 to 5' — VERIFIED. Deprecated packages detected: none new (expo-av/expo-barcode-scanner/expo-background-fetch NOT used). Pre-existing RN-Web warnings persist (shadow*/pointerEvents).

critical.issue: Returns 422 (Pydantic) for empty title/description/location/skills_required, NOT the spec'd 400 with messages 'Job Title is required.' / 'Job Description is required.' / 'Location is required.' / 'Skill Set is required.'. Root cause: JobPostBody (server.py:327-338) uses Field(min_length=2/1) which Pydantic enforces BEFORE handler runs. Fix: drop the Pydantic min_length constraints (or make fields Optional[str]=None / list=[]) so the handler's 400 branches at server.py:1368-1375 actually execute. Frontend client-side validation already catches this so UX is OK, but any non-RN consumer (curl, native iOS without client gate) will see the wrong response shape.

minor.issue: Carried over: SendGrid 401 in env; mock_otp fallback keeps tests alive but no real emails sent.

Spec mentions 'Company Name is required.' but server.py:1377-1380 auto-falls-back to poster's company/name/email — empty `company` never raises 400. If 'Company Name' is truly mandatory per iter-9, that fallback must be removed and a 400 raised.

Body uses Pydantic InterviewSlotBody; current schema does NOT make skill_set Field(min_length=1) so the handler's custom 400 'Skill Set is required.' DOES fire correctly (verified). Inconsistency with JobPostBody worth noting — pick one convention across mandatory-field guards.

issues: Carried over: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow); 'props.pointerEvents' deprecated (move into style). Non-blocking RN-Web warnings on every screen load.

action_items: (HIGH) Fix POST /api/jobs 422 vs 400: in /app/backend/server.py:327-338 remove `Field(min_length=2)` from title/description/location and `Field(min_length=1)` from skills_required (or replace with Optional[str]=None / list=[] defaults). The handler at server.py:1368-1375 already emits the correct human messages for empty values; the Pydantic constraints currently short-circuit it. After the fix, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration9.py and the 4 failing assertions (test_missing_title_400, test_missing_description_400, test_missing_location_400, test_missing_skills_required_400) should turn green for a 17/17 + 136/136 = 153/153 total.

(LOW) Clarify Company Name handling — if iter-9 mandates 'Company Name is required.' as a real 400, remove the silent fallback at server.py:1377-1380 and add a 400 guard above. Otherwise drop the 'Company Name is required.' string from the spec.
(LOW, carry-over from iter-8) Replace shadow* style props with boxShadow; move pointerEvents into style across components.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2306 lines (was 2259) — splitting into routers (auth, profile, jobs, interviews, applications, leaderboard, wallet, admin, payouts, pro_extras) is overdue.

Mandatory-field strategy is inconsistent: JobPostBody uses Pydantic Field(min_length=...) AND in-handler raises (the latter is dead code for empty values), while InterviewSlotBody relies solely on in-handler raises. Pick one — recommended: drop Pydantic length constraints on user-facing fields and put all human messages in the handler so the API returns 400 with the spec'd `detail` string consistently.

pro_missing_fields() (server.py:719-745) hard-codes 10 factor labels — recommend extracting to a module-level tuple PRO_MANDATORY_FIELDS for unit-testability and so the frontend can fetch the canonical label list from a /constants endpoint instead of duplicating in profile.tsx.

maskPhone() in profile.tsx:15-28 uses 'X'.repeat(Math.max(4, digits.length - 4)) which produces a minimum-4-X mask even for very short numbers (e.g. 5-digit input would render XXXX5 instead of X5). Edge case unlikely in practice but worth documenting.
post-job.tsx duplicates the OPEN_POSITIONS_OPTIONS list that already lives on the backend (server.py:324) — recommend exposing /api/constants/open-positions to single-source the enum and avoid drift.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration9.py (NEW — 17 iter-9 tests, 13 GREEN + 4 FAILED on the JobPost 422 bug)

success_rate.backend: 94% (149/153 — 136/136 baseline + 13/17 iter9). 4 failures are a single root cause: JobPostBody Pydantic min_length constraints short-circuit the handler's 400 branches for empty fields, returning 422 instead.
success_rate.frontend: 100% verified for iter-9 scope: post-job inline-error messages, Open Positions Picker default '1 to 5', Profile Incomplete banner + 7-of-10 missing items for fresh pro, 'Alternate Gmail (Used for Communication)' label (renders UPPERCASE via CSS), 'Your Total Experience (years)' label on slots + profile (NOT 'Candidate experience'), Gmail-verified ConfirmDialog with correct title + Verify Gmail/Cancel buttons, Verify Gmail tap routes to /professional/profile.

seed_data_creation: Throwaway @referme.io pros/students/employers + 1 @acmecorp.io pro created via /auth/signup + /auth/verify-otp during the iter-9 test run. Multiple TEST_iter9_* jobs posted by employer fixture. All entries are uniquely-suffixed (uuid4 hex) and identifiable for cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-9 backend bug to retest after main-agent fix: POST /api/jobs with empty title/description/location/skills_required must return HTTP 400 (not 422) with detail strings 'Job Title is required.' / 'Job Description is required.' / 'Location is required.' / 'Skill Set is required.'. Re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration9.py::TestJobPostingMandatoryFields -v` and expect 5/5 GREEN. Then re-run the full suite — target 153/153. Note: the iter-4 leaderboard test failure carried over from iter-8 was NOT reproducible in this iter-9 baseline (136/136 baseline GREEN this run, presumably because the test student happened to fall on page 1 today; this test is known-flaky against seed-data growth — see iter-8 RCA). Frontend smoke for iter-9 was driven via /api/auth/signup + verify-otp + UI login flow (not localStorage injection) — pro_email format `t1_iter9_<uuid6>@acmecorp.io`, password Test@12345. The UI gate, ConfirmDialog routing, post-job inline errors, profile completion banner, and Picker default '1 to 5' are all functioning. Carry-over (NOT re-verified this iter): student /sign-out Alert.alert 3-button web-preview no-op — main agent should also confirm fix landed.
rca_of_issue: JobPostBody Pydantic short-circuit RCA: In /app/backend/server.py line 327-338, JobPostBody declares `title: str = Field(min_length=2)`, `description: str = Field(min_length=2)`, `location: str = Field(min_length=2)`, `skills_required: list[str] = Field(min_length=1)`. When the FastAPI endpoint POST /api/jobs receives a JSON body with any of these set to an empty string / empty list, Pydantic raises a ValidationError DURING body parsing — before the handler body at server.py:1366 ever runs. FastAPI converts this to HTTP 422 with the standard `{detail:[{type:'string_too_short',...}]}` envelope. The handler's own validation at lines 1368-1375 (`if not body.title or len(body.title.strip()) < 2: raise HTTPException(400, 'Job Title is required.')`) is therefore unreachable for true empties; it can only fire when a string passes Pydantic min_length=2 but is whitespace-only (verified by test_skills_required_only_whitespace_400 GREEN — sending ['   '] (length-3 whitespace) passes Pydantic and triggers the 400 'Skill Set is required.'). Mitigation: change Field(min_length=2) → no constraint (and have the handler do `if not (body.title or '').strip(): raise 400 'Job Title is required.'`), OR change to Optional[str] = None and rely fully on the handler-side check. Frontend impact: app/professional/post-job.tsx validates client-side first (validate() at line 40-51) so end users never hit the 422; but any direct API consumer (curl, native client without client validation, future iOS app) WILL receive 422 and break the contract. Reproduction: `curl -X POST '<base>/api/jobs' -H 'Authorization: Bearer <employer_token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"title":"","description":"JD","location":"Bangalore","skills_required":["Java"]}'` → expected 400 'Job Title is required.', actual 422 Pydantic envelope.
